predictions.reasoning: Morning EMA (08:43) for a participant with PHQ-8=19 and high trait NA=31. Pre-EMA signals show already active: 53 min driving and 275 chars typed by 08:43, walking z=+8.06 vs personal morning baseline. Prior day (Apr 28) was very low-activity. Heavy social app use (63% of screen time) and 42 music tracks suggest mood regulation efforts consistent with anxiety/loneliness profile (low extraversion=4). No comparable historical days available. Depression and anxiety baseline strongly anchor prediction toward elevated NA with low PA. Morning timing, some physical activity, and outdoor environment provide slight positive offset, but insufficient to overcome clinical-level depressive/anxious profile.

Morning EMA (8:43) showed ER_desire=0 and positive diary mentioning beautiful weather and garden coffee — strong positive morning anchor. Day has been active and outdoors: 34min walking (Z=+63 vs baseline of 0.6min), 2h11m driving, outdoor environment, 42 music tracks, social apps dominating screen (65/103min). Sharp contrast to near-zero activity yesterday. This fits the participant's known coping profile of valuing outdoor time and nature. Despite high clinical baseline (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12, Trait NA=31), the behavioral trajectory today is consistently positive. Social app engagement + keyboard output (1555 chars) suggests social connectedness, not isolation. ER_desire expected to remain low. Limited baseline data reduces certainty.

Evening EMA after a day that started positively (garden/weather/coffee, ER=0) and had manageable afternoon work stress (ER=2, still feeling good). By 8 PM, behavioral signatures show recreational mode: 42 music tracks, outdoor environment, social apps dominating screen time (1h5m/1h43m total), active keyboard engagement (1555 chars). Screen time and walking are both extremely elevated vs this person's typical evenings (Z>40), suggesting unusual activity but in an engaged/positive direction. Low extraversion (4) makes the social app dominance meaningful — likely quality family/close contact consistent with high social support score (68). Work stress noted but not carried into evening behavioral patterns. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 suggest baseline vulnerability, but today's trajectory (positive morning → managed afternoon → active social evening) supports above-baseline PA. ER desire held low (2), INT availability likely yes given leisure evening context.

Morning EMA with no pre-EMA sensor data. Prediction anchored on: (1) strong positive carryover from April 29 evening (family time, ER_desire=0), (2) full-day profile showing sedentary indoor high-screen day with heavy social app engagement consistent with passive scrolling rather than active distress, (3) chronic depression/anxiety burden (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12, trait NA=31) maintains elevated background NA and worry even on relatively good days, (4) similar-day lookup (Apr 29 eve) confirms low ER_desire pattern, (5) morning availability pattern consistent across all April 29 EMAs. Positive affect moderately suppressed by depression baseline; negative affect present but not acute.

Morning EMA reported poor sleep (ER=3), providing a strong negative affect anchor. By afternoon, behavioral signature shows heavy social app use (3h 5m, 58% of app time) with 246 screen unlocks — consistent with anxious, distracted scrolling rather than positive social engagement, particularly given low extraversion. Music listening and unusual walking (+9.2 SD above baseline) suggest mood-regulatory coping activity. Combined with high trait NA (31), moderate-severe depression (PHQ-8=19), and moderate anxiety profile, afternoon state is likely mildly negative — sad, worried, somewhat lonely — though not extreme. ER_desire stable around 3 from morning. Indoors, sedentary context with available phone access makes intervention technically feasible.

Sleep-deprived day (noted at morning EMA, ER_desire=3) with anomalously high screen time (Z=+24.27) concentrated in earlier hours, predominantly social apps (3h 5m, 58%). This pattern suggests restless/anxious coping with poor sleep and chronic depression/anxiety (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12). Device off for 6+ hours pre-EMA implies transition to family activity — consistent with most similar past day (04/29 evening: 'with husband and daughter'). Evening context tempers acute distress but baseline NA remains elevated from sleep deprivation + high trait NA=31. Low ER desire expected (mirroring 04/29 20:05 pattern). Individual-level: NA, sadness, worry elevated above personal threshold; PA below personal threshold. Family interaction quality good, not lonely. ER desire state false — no active regulation need despite subdued mood.

Morning EMA after a positive prior evening (gardening with daughter, ER_desire=0). Device was off pre-EMA (sleeping). Outdoor environment and music engagement on the full day suggest active mood regulation. All 6 prior EMAs show INT_availability=yes. Similar days consistently show ER_desire=0 and availability. ER_desire predicted low (1) reflecting general pattern with slight uncertainty given Apr 30 morning had ER=3 after poor sleep.

Active, outward-focused afternoon: good prior sleep, outdoor environment, elevated walking (z=+5.37), heavy music listening (49 tracks), sustained social app engagement.
